name: Spain (1785–1873; 1875–1931)
description: Three horizontal stripes with the central yellow stripe twice the height of the red stripes (1:2:1), with the royal arms of Spain on the central yellow stripe near the hoist.
history: The red-yellow-red flag of Spain was adopted by Charles III on 28 May 1785, originally as a naval ensign — chosen so that Spanish ships could be distinguished from the many other naval powers using white-based flags. It became the de-jure national flag in 1843 and (with various changes to the central arms) has remained Spain's flag ever since, except for the period 1931–1939 when the Second Republic used a red-yellow-purple tricolour.
